data.body:
Brainstorm + plan-of-record for epic gnu-whale-ichor (e68d-b701-4dd9-47aa), "Authenticated identity".

OBJECTIVE (clarified by Joe): give rebar a modeled identity for the user running it, to enable (1) accurate Jira (later GitHub) user mapping, (2) optional per-project requirement of an authenticated identity attributing changes to a real user (defaulting to the GitHub committer), (3) zero-config low friction for projects that don't opt in.

CHOSEN PATTERN: adopt git-bug's identity model wholesale (it is the strongest analog — event-sourced, git-backed, multi-clone) on rebar's existing event/ticket machinery (new `identity` ticket type, event-log-as-version-chain). One coherent trust family (self-managed keys + TOFU + offline). Gap-fills vs vanilla git-bug, none foreign:
- G1 SSHSIG instead of PGP (adoption + reuse GitHub SSH key); `ssh-keygen -Y sign/verify` over canonical bytes, namespace `rebar`; shell out (no maintained pure-Python SSHSIG verifier).
- G2 implement the signed identity-chain authorization git-bug stubbed (`IsProtected()` TODO): rotate/add/revoke keys must be signed by a currently-valid key; per-person 1-of-n (Keybase topology, NOT Radicle per-repo m-of-n).
- G3 opt-in project enforcement: local write-gate + CI/fsck verify (close-gate precedent).
- G4 availability: replay NEVER rejects (vs git-bug fail-close); mark verified|unverified|unsigned, CI fails policy.
- G6 compaction survival: SNAPSHOT + process_snapshot carry folded-event identity/sig.

DE-FRANKENSTEINED (cut after adversarial subagent critique): NO DSSE/Sigstore/keyless seam (different online/CA/OIDC trust model; YAGNI; canonical-bytes boundary preserves the option). NO active GitHub ssh_signing_keys social-proof in v1 (github-login stored as plain metadata; active proof is a follow-on). Relabel: it's git-bug ENTITY + Radicle SSHSIG SUBSTRATE + Keybase per-person TOPOLOGY — Radicle proves the substrate, not the (per-repo m-of-n) topology we reject.

TRUST-ROOT convergent four-layer recipe (Radicle/TUF/Keybase/Sigstore): (a) self-certifying genesis+TOFU, (b) signed chain authorized by current key set, (c) optional external attestation, (d) equivocation detection. rebar implements all four within one trust family.

JIRA IDENTITY INTEGRATION (given ACLI): reconciler uses official `acli jira workitem` (subprocess) + direct REST (acli_rest.py) for what acli can't do. Today only assignee syncs (bare string), no reporter, no per-user binding. Official acli has NO user-lookup and NO --reporter; --assignee takes email/accountId (server-side resolve). Reporter attribution needs direct REST (fields.reporter:{accountId}) + Modify Reporter permission, else falls back to bot. accountId is the only stable id; email privacy-gated/null; /user/search is prefix-match/unreliable — so the tool MUST maintain its own map. The `identity` entity IS that map (jira-accountId immutable metadata): outbound resolve identity→accountId, set assignee (acli/REST) + reporter (REST when permitted), bootstrap/repair via /user/search, degrade to -1/unassigned/bot. Inbound reverse-resolve accountId→identity, resolve-or-create auto-upgrading ghost. Provider-neutral (jira-accountId now, GitHub node-id later) so be74 reuses it.

Tiers: T0 unverified git-config author (DCO-style, default), T1 SSHSIG opt-in. Enforcement = local write-gate + CI/fsck; replay never rejects.

Research surveyed: git-bug, Radicle/Heartwood, Pijul, Keybase, TUF, Sigstore/gitsign, Fossil, Plane, Huly, OpenProject, Taiga, sourcehut, Bugzilla, GitLab/Gitea/Forgejo; SSHSIG/allowed_signers, GitHub ssh_signing_keys (forward-only, no reverse), DSSE/securesystemslib, ACLI (official + Bob Swift/Appfire).

OUT/follow-ons: DSSE/keyless+transparency (sibling security epic 8a1c), active github-login proof, full reconciler provider-generalization + GitHub Issues user sync (be74), reporter sync without Modify Reporter (documented degradation only).
